breaking.title: West Asia war LIVE: In phone conversation with Iran Prez, PM Modi reiterates importance of freedom of navigation in Hormuz
breaking.summary: U.S., Iran pause strikes but disagree over next steps on talks; Pakistan, also a key mediator, had said talks between Iran and the U.S. would resume on June 30

breaking.title: One dead, 17 injured as naphtha pipeline catches fire in Bengal
breaking.summary: Locals say that during the early hours of the morning there was an explosion with a loud sound in the Haldia Petrochemicals pipeline.

breaking.title: WHO sounds alarm on disease outbreaks in quake-hit Venezuela
breaking.summary: By the latest official count, some 1,700 are dead and 5,000 are injured, with no governmental word on the number of missing. Other estimates place these in the tens of thousands

breaking.title: Roof collapse kills 14 children at Pakistan tuition centre
breaking.summary: Two people are taken into custody after the incident in the Kahna suburb of Lahore.

breaking.title: SP’s Chief Whip in U.P. Assembly resigns, says followed direction of party leadership
breaking.summary: The resignation is seen as an attempt by the SP to make changes in the structure of the party

breaking.title: Monsoon tracker updates: Monthly average rainfall over India expected to be below normal in July, says IMD
breaking.summary: The flood situation worsened in Arunachal Pradesh, as one more person died, taking the toll to four, while 21 others were injured in the State, where 90,499 people were affected in almost all 28 districts, the authorities said on Tuesday.

breaking.title: Russia says shot down over 400 Ukrainian drones, baby killed outside Moscow
breaking.summary: Kyiv has stepped up its long-range drone strike campaign against Russia in recent months, as Moscow's full-scale offensive against Ukraine drags on for almost 4.5 years

breaking.title: 'No direct US-Iran talks': Top US envoys reach Doha for Qatar-mediated negotiations
breaking.summary: In Doha, US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner engaged with Qatari mediators to discuss Iran negotiations. They learned from both Qatar and Iran that no direct meetings would take place between US and Iranian officials, contradicting President Trump's earlier assertions. Instead, the conversations, organized by Qatar, will prioritize implementing a memorandum of understanding, which includes addressing the release of Iran's frozen assets.
